data.title: China Traders Defy Beijing's Ban to Snap Up BHP Ore at a Steep Discount
data.description:
BHP Group has quietly moved several iron ore cargoes into China this week, despite an ongoing standoff with Beijing's powerful state buyer that could reshape one of the world's largest commodity trade routes. Traders familiar with the deals said at least four shipmentsincluding premium Mining Area C and Newman fineswere sold to both state-run and private merchants in US dollars, at discounted prices, through private tenders. The cargoes, set to load in late November and early December, come even as contract negotiations between BHP and China Mineral Resources Group (CMRG) remain stalled since September.

CMRG, established by Beijing to tighten control over global iron ore pricing, had urged Chinese mills and traders to suspend new dollar-denominated purchases from BHP and earlier discouraged sales of its Jimblebar blend fines. Yet, this week's transactions indicate some buyers are willing to test those boundaries, taking advantage of double-digit discounts to benchmark prices. While CMRG lacks formal enforcement power, its recommendations carry political weight in a sector vital to China's steel industry, and its latest curbs were meant to reinforce Beijing's push for greater pricing leverage.

Speaking at BHP's annual meeting on Thursday, Chair Ross McEwan confirmed that discussions with CMRG are ongoing, noting that similar negotiations have previously stretched for up to six months. He reiterated BHP's long-standing ties with China, describing the current tension as part of the normal commercial cycle. For now, shipments remain largely unaffected, since most of BHP's 2025 allocation is already committed. But the real pressure point could come next month, when the miner begins marketing January cargoes under the shadow of China's evolving pricing strategy.

data.title: Boston Beer Company Inc reports results for the quarter ended September 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Boston Beer Company Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $4.25​​ per share for the quarter ended September 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $5.35. The mean expectation of twelve analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $3.36 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $2.38 to $4.48 per share.

  • Revenue fell 11.2% to $537.49 million from a year ago; analysts expected $541.93 million.

  • Boston Beer Company Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $4.25​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $46.16 million.

  • Boston Beer Company Inc shares had risen by 3.4% this quarter and lost 27.1% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 13.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, three analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 11 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the brewers peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Boston Beer Company Inc is $230.00, about 4.9% above its last closing price of $218.67

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data October 23 at 10:25 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Orlando to Sell $258M of Bonds for Airport Expansion
data.description:
By Paulo Trevisani

The Greater Orlando Aviation Authority plans to issue $257.7 million of bonds to finance a major maintenance base expansion for facilities leased to United Airlines.

The tax-exempt Special Purpose Airport Facilities Revenue Bonds will fund a new 354,000 square-foot maintenance facility for United, according to a document posted Wednesday on MuniOS. The bonds will mature between 2034 and 2037, and are subject to the alternative minimum tax.

Pricing is set for Nov. 4, with closing on Nov. 13.

The project includes a hangar capable of accommodating six narrow-body aircraft, along with space for parts storage and workshops. Construction is scheduled to be completed by late 2027 at a total cost of about $290 million. United is expected to pay around $6.25 million from its own funds. The airline reported pre-tax earnings of $1.3 billion in the third quarter.

The securities are backed by payments from United to the authority.

S&P Global Ratings has assigned a rating of BB+ to the bonds.

J.P. Morgan is the lead underwriter.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Intel Shows Progress in First Earnings Report Since U.S. Investment — WSJ
data.description:
By Robbie Whelan

Intel's turnaround effort gathered momentum as it reported sales growth in its core product line of PC processors and shrinking costs across the company, the result of layoffs and other cuts that have accelerated this year.

The beleaguered chipmaker, which has fallen badly behind rivals like Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices in the race to develop powerful AI chips, reported on Thursday net income of $4.1 billion for the third quarter compared with a year-earlier loss of $16.6 billion.

Intel is burning through its inventory of PC chips faster than it had expected, as businesses rush to refresh office computers with the latest version of the Windows operating system, Chief Financial Officer David Zinsner said in an interview.

"Now we're dealing with supply," Zinsner said. "Demand is much stronger than we anticipated when we planned our supply for the year."

The result breaks Intel's longest losing streak in 35 years; the company had reported six straight quarterly losses before Thursday.

Shares rose more than 7% in after-hours trading.

The company's shares have risen more than 85% so far this year, with nearly all the gains coming since August, when the U.S. government announced that it would convert around $9 billion in federal grants into a 10% equity stake in the company.

Intel's shares soared again in September, after chip giant Nvidia — the world's largest public company with a market value of more than $4.4 trillion — said it would invest $5 billion in Intel and for the first time buy Intel's X86 CPUs, the computer brains it makes for use in data center servers.

Both deals bolstered confidence in Intel's future after a yearslong slump: the federal partnership because it suggested that Intel is now trading on the government's credit and that the administration wouldn't allow the company to fail; the Nvidia deal because it gives Intel a bigger foothold in the roaring data center market. Tech investment firm SoftBank also invested $2 billion in Intel this year.

Huge challenges remain, however. Intel Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan, who took the helm in March after four chaotic months in which the company didn't have a permanent CEO, has said his priority is revamping Intel's product lines, cutting costs and attracting more customers for the company's contract chip-manufacturing business, or foundry. Revenue in the foundry business was down 2% in the third quarter from a year earlier, at $4.2 billion.

Intel also faces stiff competition in both the PC and data center spaces, especially from AMD, which has rolled out rival products at a steady clip. Sales of Intel's data center chips were $4.1 billion for the quarter, down 1% from the year-earlier period.

Zinsner, Intel's CFO, said he expects sales of data center chips to strengthen in the coming quarters as so-called "hyperscalers" upgrade older server installations with new CPUs and as they focus more on inference functions, or the computations that allow AI models to respond to user queries.

Intel specializes in CPUs and has struggled to design GPUs — graphics processing units, which power AI training and have fueled Nvidia's explosive growth in recent years — that appeal to software developers.

As more models get trained up, however, AI companies are expected to shift their investments to the chips needed for inference and buy more CPUs, which tend to cost less than GPUs and draw much less power.

Using GPUs to power inference for smaller AI models, such as the thousands of enterprise AI tools used by businesses, "is like taking your Ferrari out to get the groceries," Zinsner said. The shift will benefit Intel's data center chips business, he said.

Once the dominant player by far in PC chips, Intel lagged far behind rivals in the AI boom and struggled to produce a return on large investments in its foundry business. A planned, $28 billion chip-manufacturing plant in Ohio has been delayed repeatedly, and last quarter Tan said its completion would be pushed back to after 2030 as the company re-evaluates its capital investments.

Intel's foundry business stemmed some bleeding this past quarter, slashing losses by more than half. The contract manufacturing business saw sales fall 2% to $4.2 billion, but the foundry's net loss was $2.3 billion, compared with $5.8 billion a year ago.

Tan continued with his aggressive cost-cutting program in the third quarter as well, reducing the number of Intel employees by 13%, from 101,400 in the second quarter to 88,400. Total company headcount, including subsidiaries, is down 29% from a year ago.

Intel forecast $12.8 billion to $13.8 billion in sales for the fourth quarter, roughly in line with the consensus estimate of $13.4 billion from analysts polled by Factset.

Write to Robbie Whelan at robbie.whelan@wsj.com

data.title: Australia Factory Activity Contracts for the 1st Time This Year
data.description:
The S&P Global Australia Manufacturing PMI fell to 49.7 in October 2025 from 51.4 in September, marking a return to contraction after nine consecutive months of expansion.

New orders for goods fell at the fastest pace so far this year, while export demand weakened after a brief uptick in August as manufacturers cited subdued global trade conditions.

Business confidence dropped to an 11-month low amid trade uncertainties and softer external demand.

Manufacturing input prices rose at the quickest pace since April, driven by higher input material and transport costs and supply disruptions, but firms reported limited ability to pass these increases on as selling price inflation moderated amid heightened competition.

data.title: With East Wing nearly demolished, White House looks for more donors to help fund ballroom
data.description:
Key points:
  • Demolition of East Wing draws criticism
  • Trump pledges millions, donors include tech giants and Winklevoss brothers
  • White House claims transparency amid concerns over review process

By Jeff Mason and Gram Slattery

The White House is looking for more donors to help fund President Donald Trump's $300 million ballroom.

The president's team has released a list of companies and wealthy individuals who have pledged to contribute to the project's cost, but it has not said how much each is giving or specified how much Trump himself intends to pony up.

The project had received nearly $200 million in pledged contributions as of last week, according to a White House official.

The president said on Thursday he intended to give millions of dollars. "I'll donate whatever's needed," he said.

Corporations including Amazon , Apple , Meta Platforms , Microsoft , Alphabet's Google, as well as individuals such as Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, have agreed to help pay for the ballroom, according to the White House.

"Fundraising has not stopped," the White House official told Reuters.

Last week, Trump addressed roughly three dozen major donors and corporate leaders at a dinner in the East Room of the White House to highlight progress on the project.

“We have a lot of legends in the room tonight, and that’s why we’re here to celebrate you, because you gave,” Trump told them.

The cost for the 90,000-square-foot addition has grown from Trump's initial $200 million estimate to $300 million this week, as demolition workers take down the entire East Wing, which held offices for the first lady and other staff. The demolition has prompted outrage from Democrats and others who were shocked at seeing part of the historic building turned to rubble.

The Department of the Interior was taking the lead on what to do with the rubble, the official said, adding he was not aware of any plans to sell pieces of demolished walls as souvenirs.

The official said demolition work could be completed in the coming days. "The goal is as soon as possible," he said.

The White House on Thursday said it had been transparent about the project despite concern expressed by critics that it did not go through a proper review process before tearing down the East Wing. Trump, when he announced the project in July, said it would not interfere with the existing structure.

"The president has been incredibly transparent," White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt told a briefing. "With any construction project there are changes over time as you assess what the project is going to look like, and we'll continue to keep you apprised of all of those changes, but just trust the process."

data.title: Mueller Water Raises Dividend to 7c Vs. 0.067c >MWA
Correct: Mueller Water Raises Dividend to 7c Vs. 6.7c >MWA
Mexican broadcaster Televisa swings to bigger-than-expected net loss in Q3
data.description:
By Brendan O'Boyle

Mexico's largest broadcaster, Grupo Televisa , swung to a third-quarter loss largely due to a non-cash deferred tax write-off, the company said in a filing to Mexico's stock exchange on Thursday.

The world's largest producer of Spanish-language content posted a net loss of 1.93 billion pesos ($105 million), far exceeding analysts' dollar-denominated forecast of a $12 million loss. It posted a net profit of 666 million pesos in the year-ago quarter.

Televisa said the swing was driven mainly by a 2.66 billion deferred tax asset write-off related to capital tax losses, as well as lower profits from associates and joint ventures, including its partnership with U.S. Spanish-language broadcaster Univision.

Revenues fell 4.8% to 14.63 billion pesos ($799 million), with Televisa's satellite television unit SKY's subscriber base dropping by about 24%, pushing revenues down 18%. Cable revenue was flat, with broadband and mobile services notching modest growth and its video segment losing about 43,000 subscribers in the quarter.

Capital expenditures reached about 3.66 billion pesos. Total debt and lease liabilities were reduced by about 16.6 billion pesos.

A modest appreciation of the Mexican peso against the U.S. dollar reduced net finance expense by about 917 million pesos, Televisa said, partially offsetting other negative impacts.

Mexico's peso strengthened 7% in the 12 months through the end of September, and just over 2% in the quarter.

Traditional broadcasters like Televisa continue to face pressure from global streaming platforms.

TelevisaUnivision said on October 1 that Google's YouTube TV had dropped Univision from its platform. TelevisaUnivision said the move stripped "millions of Hispanic viewers of the Spanish-language news, sports, and entertainment they rely on every day."

($1 = 18.3147 Mexican pesos at end-September)

data.title: Australia Services PMI Expansion Accelerates in October
data.description:
The S&P Global Flash Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index rose to 53.1 in October from 52.4 in September, indicating faster expansion in the service sector.

The improvement was driven by higher new business inflows, though growth in new orders and employment slowed compared with previous months.

Export demand for services strengthened, helping to offset manufacturing weakness and stabilise overall new export business.

Business confidence among service providers fell to an 11-month low as optimism over future output growth waned amid concerns about competition and the broader economic outlook.

On the cost side, input price inflation eased to its lowest in nearly a year, while selling price inflation fell to a five-year low as firms faced limited capacity to raise charges due to strong market competition.

data.title: Ecopetrol S.A. has obtained authorization to execute a loan with local financial institutions for COP 700,000 million, under a committed credit line structure
data.description:
BOGOTA, Colombia, Oct. 23, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Ecopetrol S.A. (; ) ("Ecopetrol" or the "Company") hereby informs the market that, through Resolution 2691 dated October 21, 2025 (the "Resolution"), the Ministry of Finance and Public Credit ("MHCP") authorized the Company to enter into a domestic loan agreement for up to COP 700,000 million with Banco Davivienda, under a non-revolving committed credit line structure. The loan is expected to have a five-year term starting from the date of the first disbursement, with a bullet repayment at maturity, and interest accruing at a variable rate indexed to the IBR rate, in accordance with the terms previously agreed with the lender. The credit line may be drawn during the 12 months following the execution date of the loan.

The proceeds from this transaction are expected to be used for non-investment expenditures, in line with the Resolution and the Company's financing plan. The Company complied with all internal procedures and approvals required for the loan. The MHCP reviewed and authorized the terms of the draft agreement, which outlines standard borrower default events such as failure to pay principal and interest, potential impairment of the borrower's repayment capacity, issues affecting the integrity of its financial information, and breach of contractual obligations, among others. Should any of these events occur, the lenders would have the right to demand early repayment of the loan, pursuant to the procedures set forth in the agreement. Likewise, the draft agreement grants Ecopetrol the right to seek recourse against the lenders in the event of non-disbursement of the loan.

The terms secured for the committed credit line, including the lowest spread rate obtained by Ecopetrol in the local market, reaffirm the support and confidence of the domestic financial sector in Ecopetrol's strategy. This is particularly relevant in the current market, where, despite challenging conditions, the Company continues to achieve favorable financing terms. Moreover, it sends a positive signal to credit rating agencies, as the committed credit line provides a reliable and flexible source of liquidity that contributes to the Company's financial stability and strengthens its cash position.

-------------------------------------

Ecopetrol is the largest company in Colombia and one of the main integrated energy companies in the American continent, with more than 19,000 employees. In Colombia, it is responsible for more than 60% of the hydrocarbon production of most transportation, logistics, and hydrocarbon refining systems, and it holds leading positions in the petrochemicals and gas distribution segments. With the acquisition of 51.4% of ISA's shares, the company participates in energy transmission, the management of real-time systems (XM), and the Barranquilla - Cartagena coastal highway concession. At the international level, Ecopetrol has a stake in strategic basins in the American continent, with Drilling and Exploration operations in the United States (Permian basin and the Gulf of Mexico), Brazil, and Mexico, and, through ISA and its subsidiaries, Ecopetrol holds leading positions in the power transmission business in Brazil, Chile, Peru, and Bolivia, road concessions in Chile, and the telecommunications sector.

This release contains statements that may be considered forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the U.S. Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. All forward-looking statements, whether made in this release or in future filings or press releases, or orally, address matters that involve risks and uncertainties, including in respect of the Company's prospects for growth and its ongoing access to capital to fund the Company's business plan, among others. Consequently, changes in the following factors, among others, could cause actual results to differ materially from those included in the forward-looking statements: market prices of oil & gas, our exploration, and production activities, market conditions, applicable regulations, the exchange rate, the Company's competitiveness and the performance of Colombia's economy and industry, to mention a few. We do not intend and do not assume any obligation to update these forward-looking statements.

Carolina Tovar Aragón

Marcela Ulloa

data.title: Annaly Capital Is Maintained at Buy by Jones Trading
Lloyds Defies the Dip: Profit Slides, Outlook Soars
data.description:
Lloyds Banking Group raised its income outlook even as profits fell short of expectations, signaling confidence in its balance sheet despite economic headwinds. The bank reported a pretax profit of 1.17 billion for the third quarter, down 36% from last year and below analysts' 1.45 billion estimate, largely due to an 800 million charge for missold motor finance. Still, Lloyds lifted its forecast for underlying net interest income to 13.6 billion this year, up from 13.5 billion, supported by its 244 billion structural hedge benefiting from higher-rate investments. CEO Charlie Nunn said the group's strong capital generation and disciplined cost control continue to underpin earnings momentum heading into year-end.

The bigger drag came from regulatory pressures. Lloyds, the UK's largest car finance lender, has now earmarked 1.95 billion in total provisions as the Financial Conduct Authority's proposed redress program expands beyond what banks had anticipated. CFO William Chalmers described the process as too early to call, but signaled that Lloyds expects a constructive dialogue with regulators. The FCA said its plan is meant to deliver fair and consistent compensation after identifying unfair commission structures in about 44% of financing agreements dating back to 2007. Barclays and other lenders have also taken additional charges tied to the same issue.

Still, analysts say Lloyds is positioning itself on firmer ground for 2026. Bloomberg Intelligence noted that with a 13.8% CET1 ratio and potential for a larger buyback beyond the 1.8 billion already expected, the lender could enter the next phase with greater balance sheet flexibility. Excluding the motor finance impact, Lloyds also raised its 2025 return-on-tangible-equity target to 14%, up 50 basis points. While management sees UK GDP growth at only 1% next year, the combination of steady margins, cautious optimism, and strong capital discipline could define Lloyds' story as one of quiet resilience in a slowing economy.

data.title: Unilever's Big U.S. Bet Is Paying Off--Here Comes the Real Shake-Up
data.description:
Unilever Plc has delivered a stronger-than-expected third quarter, signaling early traction in CEO Fernando Fernandez's turnaround playbook. The maker of Hellmann's mayonnaise reported underlying sales growth of 3.9%topping analyst forecasts of 3.7%driven by a 1.5% increase in volumes. Demand in North America remained the standout, led by high-performing categories such as Dove soap and premium products like K18 hair care and Nutrafol supplements. Shares climbed as much as 2% in early London trading, bringing Unilever's year-to-date gains to just over 3%.

Fernandez told analysts the U.S. market is outperforming by a mile, highlighting the company's edge in premium segments even as global consumer confidence softens under the weight of trade tariffs and inflation concerns. Since taking over earlier this year, Fernandez has accelerated the portfolio cleanupshedding slower brands and doubling down on categories with pricing power and consistent volume growth. The company remains ahead of schedule on its 800 million cost-saving plan through 2025, while also trimming restructuring expenses to 1.2% of turnover from 1.4%.

Unilever's biggest structural movethe spin-off of its ice cream businesshas been delayed by the U.S. government shutdown, which paused the unit's NYSE registration process. Still, the company expects completion in 2025. Latin America remains the weak spot, weighed down by currency swings and tighter household budgets, though pricing corrections in Brazil are beginning to steady sales. Fernandez reiterated the company's full-year guidance of 3% to 5% underlying sales growth, noting that the turnaround's next phase will hinge on restoring momentum in emerging markets while sustaining premium demand in the U.S.

data.title: Comfort Systems USA Inc reports results for the quarter ended September 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Comfort Systems USA Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $8.25​​ per share for the quarter ended September 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $4.09. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $6.23 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $5.65 to $6.60 per share.

  • Revenue rose 35.2% to $2.45 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $2.16 billion.

  • Comfort Systems USA Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $8.25​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $291.62 million.

  • Comfort Systems USA Inc shares had fallen by 4.2% this quarter and gained 86.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 22.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the construction & engineering peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Comfort Systems USA Inc is $795.00, about 4.3% above its last closing price of $829.36

data.title: LSEG Soars 9% After 850 Million Deal With Global Banks Ignites Investor Frenzy
data.description:
London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) just delivered the kind of news that wakes up a sleepy market. Shares surged as much as 9% in early London tradingthe biggest intraday jump in over seven monthsafter the company unveiled a 170 million deal to sell a 20% stake in its Post Trade Solutions business to 11 major banks, valuing the unit at 850 million. The buyer list reads like a who's who of global financeBank of America, Barclays, BNP Paribas, Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Nomura, Societe Generale, and UBS. Alongside the sale, CEO David Schwimmer doubled down on shareholder returns, announcing a repeat of the 1 billion buyback already completed over the past three months by February next year.

Behind the headlines, the financial engine is still humming, albeit a touch slower. Annual subscription growth came in at 5.6% for the quarter through Septemberdown slightly from 6% a year agowhile total income matched forecasts at 2.22 billion. LSEG also guided margins toward the top of its target range. But the broader backdrop is brutal: London's IPO market has plunged to a 35-year low, with the exchange tumbling out of the world's top 20 venues for new listings as fundraising migrates to places like Mexico and Singapore.

Even so, Schwimmer's bet on data and AI could be LSEG's trump card. The firm is integrating its proprietary datasets into Microsoft's 365 Copilot and agentic AI tools, part of a partnership born in 2022 that may reshape how clients consume market intelligence. Schwimmer was quick to underline that LSEG's data remains tightly controlledAI, he said, can't simply replicate its value or access its real-time feeds. The Post Trade deal also resets revenue-sharing terms in its SwapClear platform, with founding banks' surplus share shrinking from 30% to 10% by 2026 in exchange for 1.15 billion in cash and up to 200 million in performance-linked payments. The move, expected to lift margins in the markets division, signals one thing: LSEG isn't waiting for the market to turnit's building its next chapter now.

data.title: Grades mixed on second day of volatile roll trade period
data.description:
Physically traded U.S. crude grades were mixed on Thursday, dealers said, on the first full day of the volatile roll trading period.

Traders use the three-day roll period to adjust their crude slates, square up positions and net out exposures following the expiration of the U.S. crude futures contract.

Prices to roll U.S. crude oil futures positions from November to December traded at a midpoint of 80 cents a barrel.

The spread between West Texas Intermediate crude futures and global benchmark Brent widened, which typically makes U.S. grades more attractive to foreign buyers.

The discount (WTCLc1-LCOc1) widened to as much as minus $4.24 a barrel. A discount of around $4 suggests an open arbitrage window.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet (WTC-LLS) for November rose 10 cents to a midpoint of a $1.63 premium and was seen bid and offered between a 75-cent and $2.50 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

  • Mars Sour (WTC-MRS) fell 5 cents to a midpoint of a $1.50 discount and was seen bid and offered between a $1.60 and $1.40 a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

  • WTI Midland (WTC-WTM) rose 15 cents to a midpoint of a 40-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 30-cent and 50-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

  • West Texas Sour (WTC-WTS) rose 8 cents to a midpoint of a 53-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a 75-cent and 30-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

  • WTI at East Houston (WTC-MEH), also known as MEH, traded between a 65-cent and 85-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

  • ICE Brent December futures rose $3.40 to settle at $65.99 a barrel.

  • WTI December crude futures rose $3.29 to settle at $61.79 a barrel.

  • The Brent/WTI spread (WTCLc1-LCOc1) widened 6 cents to last trade at minus $4.15, after hitting a high of minus $3.99 and a low of minus $4.24
